skill.name: pexo-agent
skill.description: AI video generation skill with auto model selection across Seedance 2, Kling 3.0, HappyHorse, and 10+ models. Produces finished multi-shot videos (5–120s) from text, images, URLs, scripts, or audio — including AI music, lip sync, and multi-shot sequencing. No prompts to write, no models to choose. USE FOR: video production, AI video, make a video, product video, brand video, promotional clip, explainer video, short video, TikTok video, Instagram Reel, YouTube Short, product ad, text-to-video, image-to-video, video generation, AI video agent.
skill.summary: AI video generation agent that auto-selects the best model and produces finished multi-shot videos from natural language.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Evaluation of 537 static findings confirms the skill is a legitimate API wrapper for the Pexo video generation service. The vast majority of findings are false positives expected for any shell-based API client: shell command substitution in bash scripts, access to the documented config file at ~/.pexo/config, and references to the PEXO_API_KEY environment variable. Network traffic is restricted to the user-configured PEXO_BASE_URL (defaults to pexo.ai). 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' findings are md5sum calls used for file integrity checks of downloaded assets, not security-sensitive operations. The 'dangerous combination' heuristic is a standard pattern for authenticated API client skills and poses no concrete risk. No data exfiltration, obfuscation, or prompt injection detected.

low_findings.title: Shell scripts with command substitution
low_findings.description: Scripts use shell command substitution (e.g., $(command)) for standard operations like jq parsing, curl calls, and temp file handling. This is expected for bash-based API clients. No user input is injected unsanitized into these commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All command substitutions are for legitimate API operations (jq, curl, awk). No injection vectors identified in the script flow.

low_findings.title: Hidden file access to ~/.pexo/config
low_findings.description: Scripts read the documented configuration file at ~/.pexo/config which contains PEXO_BASE_URL and PEXO_API_KEY. This is the intended config path described in SKILL.md and is not a hidden operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The config path is explicitly documented in SKILL.md as the user's expected config location. Access is limited to sourcing the file for environment variables.

low_findings.title: API key transmission to configured endpoint
low_findings.description: PEXO_API_KEY is sent as a Bearer token to the PEXO_BASE_URL endpoint. The base URL is user-configurable and defaults to pexo.ai. This is the documented API authentication pattern.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard Bearer token authentication to a user-configured API endpoint. No exfiltration to unexpected domains detected.

low_findings.title: MD5 checksums for file integrity
low_findings.description: Scripts use md5sum for computing checksums of downloaded video assets. This is a non-security-critical integrity check, not a cryptographic security operation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: MD5 is acceptable for file integrity verification (detecting corruption), which is its actual use case here. Not used for password hashing or digital signatures.

content.user_title: Generate finished videos with AI in Claude Code
content.value_statement: Making professional videos requires choosing models, writing prompts, and editing footage. Pexo Agent lets you describe what you want in plain language and delivers a finished video with music and subtitles.
actual_capabilities: Creates finished multi-shot videos (5–120 seconds) from text descriptions, images, URLs, scripts, or audio files
Auto-selects the best video generation model per shot across Seedance 2, Kling 3.0, HappyHorse, and 10+ models
Produces videos with AI music, TTS narration, lip sync, and subtitles already included
Supports 16:9 landscape, 9:16 vertical, and 1:1 square aspect ratios for any platform
Handles the full pipeline: project creation, asset upload, chat-based direction, status polling, and delivery
limitations: Requires a Pexo API key (PEXO_API_KEY) configured in ~/.pexo/config
Video generation takes 8–20 minutes depending on length and complexity
Each message to Pexo costs tokens from your credit balance
Cannot crawl web URLs directly — files must be downloaded and uploaded manually

use_cases.title: Product video ads from a photo
use_cases.description: Upload a product photo and get a finished promotional video suitable for social media ads.

use_cases.title: Short-form social content
use_cases.description: Describe a concept in text and receive a vertical video ready for TikTok, Instagram Reels, or YouTube Shorts.
use_cases.target_user: Content creators and social media managers

use_cases.title: Explainer videos with narration
use_cases.description: Provide a script and get a video with TTS voiceover, visuals, and subtitles assembled automatically.

prompt_templates.title: Simple product video
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 15-second product video for my [product name]. It should be energetic and modern, with upbeat music.
prompt_templates.scenario: Quick social media ad from a single product image

prompt_templates.title: Brand story with multiple shots
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 60-second brand video for [company name]. Show our workshop, our team, and a close-up of our craftsmanship. Use warm cinematic tones.
prompt_templates.scenario: Multi-shot brand video with consistent visual style

prompt_templates.title: Script-to-video with narration
prompt_templates.prompt: Here is my script: [paste script]. Create a 45-second explainer video with professional narration and clean motion graphics style.
prompt_templates.scenario: Explainer or educational content with voiceover

prompt_templates.title: Revision request
prompt_templates.prompt: Make the colors more vibrant, shorten the intro to 2 seconds, and add a call-to-action text at the end saying 'Shop now'.
prompt_templates.scenario: Iterating on an existing project with specific feedback

output_examples.input: User says: "做一个猫的视频" (make a cat video) and has no files to upload
output_examples.output: The agent creates a new project, sends the message to Pexo verbatim, and replies: "Video request submitted to Pexo. Estimated time: 15–20 minutes. Project link: https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123"

output_examples.input: User uploads shoes.jpg and says: "I want a product video for my shoes"
output_examples.output: The agent uploads the image, then sends: "I want a product video for my shoes <original-image>asset_xyz</original-image>" and provides the project link with estimated completion time.

best_practices: Relay the user's exact words to Pexo — do not add your own creative direction or prompt engineering
Poll pexo-project-get.sh every 60 seconds during WAIT status; never call pexo-chat during WAIT to avoid duplicate production
Send the full video URL as plain text with all query parameters when delivering the finished video
anti_patterns: Do not add duration, aspect ratio, or style details the user did not mention — let Pexo decide based on its expertise
Do not call pexo-chat.sh while the project status is WAIT — it triggers duplicate video production and wastes credits
Do not truncate or markdown-wrap the delivery URL — missing query parameters cause 403 errors

faq.question: How long does it take to generate a video?
faq.answer: A 15-second 3-shot video takes about 8 minutes. A 60-second brand video takes about 20 minutes. Longer or more complex projects may take up to 30 minutes.

faq.question: Do I need to choose which AI model to use?
faq.answer: No. Pexo auto-selects the best model for each shot from its library including Seedance 2, Kling 3.0, and HappyHorse. You only describe what you want.

faq.question: What input formats are supported?
faq.answer: You can provide text descriptions, images (jpg, png, webp), videos (mp4, mov), audio (mp3, wav), or scripts. URLs are not crawled directly — download the file first.

faq.question: How do I set up the API key?
faq.answer: Create a file at ~/.pexo/config with PEXO_BASE_URL and PEXO_API_KEY. Run pexo-doctor.sh to verify your setup. Full instructions are in references/SETUP-CHECKLIST.md.

faq.question: Can I revise a video after it is delivered?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the same project_id and send your feedback via pexo-chat.sh. Pexo will iterate on the existing project rather than starting over.

faq.question: What aspect ratios are available?
faq.answer: 16:9 for landscape (YouTube, websites), 9:16 for vertical (TikTok, Reels, Shorts), and 1:1 for square (Instagram feed). Pexo picks the right one or you can specify.
